title: Correct a suite where one async test leaks data into another
prompt: Given this description: `test/my_app/accounts_test.exs` has 3 tests. Test 2 modifies a shared ETS table via `MyApp.Cache.put(:rate_limit, 100)`. Test 3 reads from the same ETS table and expects default value. When run async, Test 3 sometimes sees Test 2's mutation. Write `docs/ets_leakage_fix.md` describing: (1) the root cause (ETS is global, not sandbox-scoped), (2) three fix options — (a) mark this module async: false with a comment, (b) use `Process.put/2` per-test if local scope suffices, (c) use a named ETS table per test with `:ets.new/2`, (3) recommend option (a) as safest. Must NOT recommend seeding or disabling async as a blanket fix.
tier_rationale: Three named fix options with correct recommendation. Requires knowing ETS is process-agnostic.
